title: In /Users/masayuki/Dev/cursorvers/claude-config, verify test coverage/evidence for the Phase 0 handoff pipeline change. Compare against the 7-item test plan and acceptance criteria in ~/.claude/cache/handoff-phase0-codex.md and ~/.claude/cache/dev-spec-phase0-handoff-pipeline.md. Return missing test...
summary_text: **Findings (missing integrity paths first)** - **Critical: trusted quorum is still caller-asserted, not artifact-attested.** `record-provider` marks entries trusted purely from `provider+provenance` string, so a manual call can still mint trusted evidence if it uses allowed provenance values. [kernel-runtime-ledger.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-ledger.sh#L44) [kernel-runtime-ledger.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-ledger.sh#L241) [kernel-runtime-ledger.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-ledger.sh#L268) - **High: health/consensus trust trusted-counts without validating envelope/log provenance chain.** They consume trusted counts, but do not require run-driver envelope/log linkage for provider success evidence. [kernel-runtime-health.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh#L153) [kernel-runtime-health.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-health.sh#L203) [kernel-consensus-evidence.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-consensus-evidence.sh#L92) [kernel-consensus-evidence.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-consensus-evidence.sh#L292) - **High: existing artifacts are present but not used as attestation source.** Run-driver already emits workspace receipt + envelope + trace/log pointers, but trusted-provider gates do not verify against them. [kernel-runtime-run-driver.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-run-driver.sh#L215) [kernel-runtime-run-driver.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-run-driver.sh#L325) [kernel-runtime-workspace.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-runtime-workspace.sh#L130) - **Medium: test gap on spoofed “trusted provenance.”** Current negative tests only prove bare manual writes are untrusted; they do not test forged manual writes using allowed provenance labels (`run-driver`/`glm-exec`/`optional-lane-exec`). [test-kernel-consensus-evidence.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-consensus-evidence.sh#L61) [test-kernel-runtime-health.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-kernel-runtime-health.sh#L150) - **Workflow risk:** this leaves a path where single-lane/manual fallback can be dressed up as multi-agent success by forged trusted provenance writes, which is a workflow-integrity violation. **Smallest practical receipt-backed attestation contract** - **Contract name:** `provider-attestation-v1` (run-level, receipt-backed). - **Source of truth chain per run:** `workspace receipt -> compact artifact -> run-envelope -> run-driver log`. - **Trusted success rule:** count provider success only if all pass: 1. Evidence entry has allowed provider/provenance pair. 2. Workspace receipt exists and `run_id` matches. 3. Compact artifact exists, `run_id` matches, and `workspace_receipt_path` matches receipt. 4. Run-envelope exists under workspace traces, with `run_id` match, `status=completed`, `scheduler_state=terminal`, `exit_code=0`. 5. Envelope `log_path` exists and contains machine-readable success marker for that provider. - **Marker format (minimal):** add deterministic lines in launch quorum/log stream, e.g. `KERNEL_ATTEST lane_success provider=<name> run_id=<id>`. - **Counting change:** `trusted-provider-success-count` and `trusted-specialist-success-count` should compute from validated attestations, not raw `trusted_success_count` integers. - **Minimal implementation shape:** keep `record-provider` for raw audit, but make trusted gating derive from attestation validation over existing artifacts; no new storage system required. **Adversarial test ideas** 1. Manual spoof with trusted provenance string (`record-provider codex success ... run-driver`) but no valid envelope/log m...
